hypothesis: Including estimated heating energy (derived from reactant and product heat capacities), kinetic activation barriers, and downstream purification costs into the workflow's economic-efficiency metric will make the Ca3SiO5+SiO2 route more cost-effective than the CaO+SiO2 route for Ca2SiO4 synthesis whenever the market price of CaO rises by more than 150% above the paper's reported average or when process energy cost exceeds 0.05 €/MJ.
